mechanism_narrative: Near-zero federal funds rates reduce discount rates for equity valuations, generating positive forward S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in >55% of observations when FEDFUNDS < 0.5%, or rolling Rank IC < 0.02 over 3 years.

mechanism_narrative: Elevated unemployment rates signal weak aggregate demand and deteriorating corporate earnings, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in >60% of instances when UNRATE > 7.0%, or t-stat for UNRATE coefficient is insignificant (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in real sales of autos and light trucks exceeding 2% indicates a sharp contraction in consumer durables spending, forecasting negative equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns are positive in >50% of triggered months, or the threshold effect vanishes when RSAFS decline is between 1% and 2%.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production dropping below the 95.0 index threshold reflects severe capacity underutilization and supply-side weakness, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in >65% of observations when INDPRO < 95.0, or the threshold effect is statistically indistinguishable from a linear specification.

mechanism_narrative: The joint occurrence of rising unemployment alongside persistently low federal funds rates triggers aggressive central bank intervention expectations, generating positive forward equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The interaction term between UNRATE and FEDFUNDS yields a negative or insignificant coefficient in multivariate regression, or forward returns are negative in >55% of joint-trigger instances.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous acceleration in consumer price inflation and a rising federal funds rate signals a tightening monetary response that suppresses aggregate demand, forecasting negative equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The product of CPIAUCSL growth and FEDFUNDS changes shows no predictive power for 60-day returns, or returns are positive in >50% of high-inflation/high-rate observations.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding real GDP growth combined with accelerating housing starts signals a synchronized broad-based economic recovery, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in >60% of observations during recovery phases, or the predictive signal disappears when GDPC1 growth is below 2.0% annually.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between declining industrial production and rising total nonfarm payrolls indicates labor hoarding and deteriorating productivity, forecasting negative equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in >55% of divergence instances, or the productivity gap metric fails to outperform a simple moving average benchmark in walk-forward tests.
